description_long: This object is a stack of six hardcover books, each with distinct cover designs and colors. The top book features a grayscale photograph of two people, with a white and orange color scheme. The spines of the books display a variety of colors, including red, black, yellow, and blue, with visible text and some graphic elements. The books are of varying thicknesses, and the edges of the pages are slightly textured and uneven, suggesting frequent handling. The bottom book is the largest, with a dark cover and bold, contrasting text. The overall appearance is that of well-used, modern books, likely found in an urban environment such as a sidewalk, park bench, or bus stop.
description: A stack of six colorful hardcover books with varied cover designs and visible text, showing signs of regular use.
description_view_0: The stack is seen from the side, highlighting the textured page edges and the thickness of each book.
description_view_1: The front covers and spines are visible, displaying vibrant colors, text, and some graphic elements.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the stack shows the page edges and the layered arrangement of the books.
description_view_3: The stack is viewed from an angle, revealing both the spines and partial covers, with a focus on the book thicknesses.
attribute_cover_illustration: The main illustration on the top book's cover is a grayscale photograph of two people, possibly in a candid or documentary style. The rest of the cover is mostly plain with some colored accents. Other books in the stack have minimal or no visible illustrations.
attribute_title_text: Sufrion Alexander El Jazz de la Fisica
attribute_spine_design: The spines feature a mix of bold and regular fonts in various colors, including red, black, yellow, and white, with some graphic elements and horizontal orientation. The text is clear and prominent, aiding identification.
